mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending growth and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of observations when UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand recovery, which forces equity valuations higher over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when either RSAFS or HOUST declines, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the index of industrial production indicates supply-side bottlenecks and manufacturing weakness, which depresses corporate profit margins and generates negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of periods where INDPRO declines for two consecutive months, or if the predictive coefficient flips sign during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate earnings and prompt anticipatory monetary tightening, which systematically reduces S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.015 across all rolling 12-month windows, or 5-day forward returns show no negative correlation with CPIAUCSL growth rates (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When real gross domestic product growth exceeds 3.0 percent, strong economic momentum lifts corporate revenue forecasts and equity risk premiums compress, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails if 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 3.0 percent, or if the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and stagnant unemployment rates indicates labor market tightness that accelerates wage inflation, forcing discount rates higher and depressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns remain positive when PAYEMS rises while UNRATE stays flat.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low federal funds rates signals accommodative monetary policy successfully stimulating manufacturing output, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if 20-day forward returns are negative in over 55 percent of observations when INDPRO rises during low-rate environments, or if the regime-specific coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).
